title: EU Digital Services Act and Digital Markets Act — AI-Relevant Provisions
official_title: Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (DSA) and Regulation (EU) 2022/1925 (DMA) — provisions governing algorithmic systems and AI
overview: The Digital Services Act (DSA) and Digital Markets Act (DMA) create significant obligations for AI-driven recommendation systems, content moderation algorithms, and platform ranking systems. While they predate the EU AI Act and are not AI-specific laws, their provisions directly regulate algorithmic systems at scale. Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) and Very Large Online Search Engines (VLOSEs) face the most onerous obligations; all online intermediaries have baseline requirements.

dsa_key_provisions.title: Recommender system transparency
dsa_key_provisions.threshold: All online platforms with recommendation systems
dsa_key_provisions.description: Platforms using recommender systems must explain the main parameters used in the system and offer users at least one option not based on profiling.
obligations: Publish clear, plain-language explanations of the main parameters used in recommendation algorithms
Offer at least one recommendation option not based on profiling of the user
Make the non-profiling option easily accessible to users

dsa_key_provisions.title: Systemic risk assessment and mitigation (VLOPs/VLOSEs)
dsa_key_provisions.threshold: Very Large Online Platforms (>45M monthly active EU users) and Very Large Online Search Engines
dsa_key_provisions.description: VLOPs and VLOSEs must identify, analyse, and mitigate systemic risks arising from their algorithmic systems, including recommendation algorithms, content moderation AI, and advertising systems.
obligations: Conduct annual systemic risk assessment covering: dissemination of illegal content, adverse effects on fundamental rights, civic discourse and elections, public health, gender-based violence
Implement reasonable mitigation measures proportionate to identified risks
Independent audit of risk assessment and mitigation measures annually
Report systemic risk assessment results to the Digital Services Coordinator and European Commission

dsa_key_provisions.title: Recommender system transparency for VLOPs
dsa_key_provisions.description: VLOPs must provide additional recommender system transparency including at least one option based on explicit criteria not relying on profiling.
obligations: Provide at least one recommender option based solely on explicit user criteria (recency, topic)
Make these options accessible from the interface where content is recommended
Explain all available recommender system options clearly

dsa_key_provisions.title: Data access for researchers
dsa_key_provisions.description: VLOPs must provide vetted researchers access to data necessary to assess systemic risks from algorithmic systems.
obligations: Establish a mechanism to provide data access to vetted researchers approved by Digital Services Coordinators
Data must be sufficient to investigate systemic risks from algorithms
Cannot impose unnecessary restrictions on data access

dsa_key_provisions.title: Notice and action mechanisms; statement of reasons
dsa_key_provisions.description: When AI-based content moderation restricts content or accounts, platforms must notify affected users and provide reasons. Users must be able to challenge AI content moderation decisions.
obligations: Notify users when AI content moderation restricts their content or account
Provide clear explanation of the decision including reference to the specific rule applied
Offer internal complaint mechanism for challenging AI moderation decisions
Cooperate with out-of-court dispute settlement bodies

dma_key_provisions.title: Self-preferencing prohibition affects ranking AI
dma_key_provisions.threshold: Gatekeepers (Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, ByteDance designated)
dma_key_provisions.description: Gatekeepers cannot use their own-brand products and services more favourably in ranking than those of third parties. Applies to algorithmic ranking in app stores, search engines, and marketplaces.
obligations: Ranking algorithms cannot systematically favour gatekeeper's own services
Ranking criteria must be applied equally to own-brand and third-party services
Cannot use non-public data generated by third parties to train ranking models that benefit own services

dma_key_provisions.title: No use of third-party data for own AI models
dma_key_provisions.description: Gatekeepers cannot use data obtained from business users to develop competing products or train AI models that benefit their own competing services.
obligations: Separate data collected from business users on the platform from data used to train AI models for competing services
Obtain consent before using business user data for AI training
Document and audit data flows between platform operations and AI model training

dma_key_provisions.title: Consent requirements for cross-service AI profiling
dma_key_provisions.description: Gatekeepers cannot combine personal data across their services for advertising AI unless they obtain valid GDPR consent.
obligations: Obtain explicit, informed consent before combining personal data across different services for AI-based advertising
Provide genuine opt-out option that does not degrade core service
Cannot make consent a condition of service access

dsa_violations.note: Up to 6% global annual turnover for VLOPs/VLOSEs

dsa_repeated_violations.note: Periodic penalty payments up to 5% of average daily turnover; structural remedies for systemic infringements

dma_violations.note: Up to 10% global annual turnover; up to 20% for repeated infringements within 8 years

interaction_with_eu_ai_act: DSA systemic risk assessments for VLOPs overlap with EU AI Act risk assessments for high-risk AI systems — both may apply to the same AI-driven content moderation or recommendation system
Content moderation AI used by VLOPs may qualify as high-risk AI under EU AI Act Annex III if used for law enforcement-related purposes
DSA researcher data access provisions create obligations that interact with EU AI Act technical documentation requirements

cross_references.relevance: AI Act obligations apply in addition to DSA/DMA; VLOP recommendation systems and content moderation AI face obligations under both simultaneously.
DSA/DMA consent requirements reference GDPR consent; all cross-service profiling and advertising AI must comply with GDPR lawful basis requirements.
